strengths: long context for big retrieval bundles
JSON mode reliable for citation schemas
10x cheaper than GPT-4o for comparable reasoning
risks: lighter safety alignment than Claude/GPT-4 — needs constitutional prompts in clinical/legal/financial
training cutoff ~2024-Q3 — needs RAG for current events
generator_model.recommended_role: generator only; pair with dedicated embedder + reranker

projects.current_stack: Gemma 4 on Azure AI + ada-002 + Azure Cognitive Search

sources: user-authored Q&A pairs (local fixture, not committed)
corpus.license: Open Government Licence v3.0 (NICE), Crown copyright (BNF)

ml_layers.generator: deepseek-v3 · temperature 0.1 · json_mode for citation schema
guardrails: constitutional system prompt with NICE-citation requirement
confidence threshold ≥ 0.70 or escalate to clinician review
ml_layers.evaluation: LangSmith + inspect_petri trace logging + monthly clinician audit

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Use only behind a constitutional + mandatory-review gate. Cost win is real ($/M ≈ 1/10 of GPT-4o) but the safety alignment gap matters in clinical. Acceptable if every output carries the mandatory_human_review flag and a confidence band.
strengths_for_project: JSON mode produces clean SNOMED-code citations
risks_for_project: less aggressive refusal on borderline queries
mitigations: log every response to immutable audit trail

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are a clinical-evidence retrieval assistant for qualified NHS clinicians. Every answer MUST cite NICE guideline reference + SNOMED CT code. If evidence is insufficient, respond 'INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE'. Output JSON: {answer, citations[{nice_ref, snomed_code, confidence}], requires_human_review: true}.

projects.current_stack: XGBoost predictor + Neo4j graph + Gemma 4 chat

sources: OMOP CDM patient summaries (synthetic CPRD Gold cohort)
user-authored Q&A pairs (local fixture, not committed)

ml_layers.generator: deepseek-v3 — explains XGBoost SHAP outputs in plain English
guardrails: no individual patient PII echoed back (validation step)
UK GDPR Art.22 disclaimer appended to every response
ml_layers.evaluation: equalized-odds drift checks on cohort + qualitative clinician review

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Good fit. The RAG layer is explanatory (not diagnostic) — DeepSeek V3's reasoning translates SHAP/IMD/risk numbers into clinician-readable narratives well. Cost matters at scale (every cohort traversal triggers ~3 LLM calls).
strengths_for_project: narrative explanation of structured ML outputs
long context fits full SHAP table + cohort summary
risks_for_project: may over-confidently narrate edge-case risk scores

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are a patient-disengagement risk explainer for GP practice staff. Given an XGBoost risk score and SHAP contributors, explain in plain English what drives the score and what next-step the NICE pathway recommends. NEVER diagnose. Output JSON: {explanation, top_drivers[], suggested_pathway_ref, confidence_band}.

corpus.license: client-confidential — never push to public repos

ml_layers.embedder: text-embedding-3-large — recall matters for compliance matrix coverage
ml_layers.retriever: hybrid + section-aware filter (technical vs pricing vs Q&A)
ml_layers.generator: deepseek-v3 for first-pass drafting · claude-opus for judge/rewrite
guardrails: no real client data echoed in public-facing logs
compliance-matrix coverage check before any output
wMBE/Section 3 narrative legitimacy judge gates publish

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Strong primary generator for cost-sensitive draft passes. The 16+ judge pipeline already catches outputs that drift; DeepSeek V3 at $0.27/1M in saves ~$50/proposal vs running Opus end-to-end. Keep Claude for the final-pass technical/pricing judge calls where safety + nuance matter.
strengths_for_project: draft length (DeepSeek can output a 10-page technical section in one call)
risks_for_project: less polished prose than Claude — judges must catch awkward phrasing
weaker at preserving exact RFP-section numbering on long docs
mitigations: section-rewrite-evaluator pass after every DeepSeek draft
Claude does the final pricing-math + compliance-matrix validation

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are a government-proposal draft generator. Use ONLY the retrieved past-performance and template context. Every claim must trace to a citation_id from the corpus. Output the section in the cookbook XML schema: <thoughts/><draft/><citations/>.

projects.current_stack: Claude SDK judges + DAX templates + Power BI Fabric REST

sources: DAX measure library + perf anti-patterns docs
corpus.license: MIT (own code) + MS docs (fair use citation)

ml_layers.generator: deepseek-v3 — narrates AUDIT.md findings + suggests rewrites
guardrails: no real RLS-filtered data echoed in audit summaries
deterministic judges always win ties against LLM judges

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Good fit for the human-readable AUDIT.md narrative generation. Existing Claude judges stay as-is; DeepSeek V3 powers the optional 'explain this finding in plain English' RAG layer that translates judge XML into operator briefings.
strengths_for_project: cheap enough to run on every PR
long context fits full AUDIT.md + dashboard_spec
risks_for_project: could miss subtle DAX-syntax nuance compared to Claude — keep DAX-judge calls on Claude
mitigations: DeepSeek does narration only, never the structured judging

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are an audit-narrative explainer. Given an AUDIT.md and dashboard_spec.yml, write a 3-paragraph operator brief: what passed, what to tighten, what to re-work. Cite the specific judge_id and finding_id for every claim.

projects.current_stack: Lambda + Postgres + various data feeds (Schwab, FRED, GDELT)

ml_layers.reranker: Cohere rerank for cross-encoder precision on macro queries
ml_layers.generator: deepseek-v3 for cost · GPT-4o for high-stakes earnings interpretation
guardrails: no trade recommendations (compliance — 'analysis not advice' disclaimer)
every claim traces to filing section + page
ml_layers.evaluation: FinBERT sentiment alignment check + manual analyst spot-check

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Strong cost-efficient generator for the bulk of analyst Q&A. Long context fits a full 10-K section + macro context in one shot. Reserve GPT-4o for earnings-call-interpretation queries where numerical reasoning + nuance must be tightest.
strengths_for_project: cheap enough for high-volume analyst queries
JSON mode for structured filing-fact extraction
risks_for_project: weaker on inferential earnings-quality scoring vs GPT-4o
mitigations: route earnings-call queries to GPT-4o; route filing summarization to DeepSeek

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are an SEC-filing retrieval assistant. Every numerical claim cites <document_type>/<section>/<page>. Refuse trade recommendations. Output JSON: {summary, citations[], confidence, disclaimer: 'analysis not advice'}.

ml_layers.retriever: vector top_k=4 — small enough for full-corpus retrieval per query
ml_layers.generator: deepseek-v3 — answers 'why is ICB X high-risk?' Q&A
guardrails: aggregated stats only — no practice-level identification

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Fine for a Q&A overlay on the map. Low-volume usage; cost barely matters. DeepSeek V3 acceptable; even cheaper options (Phi-4) would also work.

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are an NHS ICB risk explainer. Given an ICB code + region stats, answer the user's question grounded in CQC + IMD + Core20PLUS5 context. Aggregated stats only — never name individual practices.

sources: NICE Evidence Standards Framework (ESF) Tier B docs

ml_layers.embedder: text-embedding-3-large — high recall on regulatory citation needed
guardrails: no clinical advice — process advice only

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Good for the human-readable audit-report narration. The structured fairness metrics (equalized-odds, demographic-parity) come from Fairlearn; DeepSeek V3 explains them against the regulatory framework references.

projects.system_prompt_pattern: You are a model-fairness audit-report writer. Given Fairlearn metrics + protected-attribute breakdowns, write the audit body section. Cite NICE ESF Tier B + Core20PLUS5 + MHRA GMLP for every recommendation.

projects.rag_applicability_note: Strategy framework — limited RAG fit. Optional: indicator-docs Q&A bot. Skip unless user explicitly wants it.

deepseek_v3_fit.verdict: Skip. Not the right project for RAG.

shared_infrastructure_recommendation.vector_store_default: Cosmos DB asi.kb_chunks (already deployed; cross-project) OR pgvector for new projects

generator_routing.gpt-4o: tool-heavy agentic flows, vision, earnings interpretation

shared_infrastructure_recommendation.auth_path: Azure Foundry serverless API (single endpoint, model-routed via deployment name) OR GitHub Models for dev/zero-cost prototyping
shared_infrastructure_recommendation.monitoring: LangSmith for trace logging across all projects + custom judge harness for project-specific quality gates

rollout_order_recommendation.rationale: Highest cost-savings ratio (DeepSeek draft → Claude judge); existing eval harness catches quality regressions; immediate ROI
Already has judge pipeline; adding DeepSeek as narration layer is low-risk
Big corpus, high query volume — cost savings compound; FinBERT specialization stays as-is
Explainer role suits DeepSeek; lower regulatory risk than clinical-rag
Last — needs the most safety scaffolding before swapping Gemma 4. Pilot in shadow mode (log both Gemma + DeepSeek outputs, compare) before any production swap
Lower-volume; bundle together after main flows are stable
